heading: Appointment and tenure of office of Surgeon General; reversion in rank
label: 205 Appointment and tenure of office of Surgeon General; reversion in rank
text: The Surgeon General shall be appointed from the Regular Corps for a four-year term by the President by and with the advice and consent of the Senate. The Surgeon General shall be appointed from individuals who (1) are members of the Regular Corps, and (2) have specialized training or significant experience in public health programs. Upon the expiration of such term the Surgeon General, unless reappointed, shall revert to the grade and number in the Regular Corps or Ready Reserve Corps that he would have occupied had he not served as Surgeon General.
notes: Amendments2020— substituted “Regular Corps or Ready Reserve Corps” for “Regular or Reserve Corps”.Pub. L. 116–1361981— inserted reference to Reserve Corps and substituted provisions relating to appointment of an individual from the Regular Corps and with specialized training and significant experience, for provisions relating to appointment of an individual sixty-four years of age or older.Pub. L. 97–35inserted provision that the President may appoint to office of Surgeon General an individual who is sixty-four years of age or older.Pub. L. 97–25

Change of Name“Secretary of Health and Human Services” substituted for “Secretary of Health, Education, and Welfare” pursuant to , which is classified to , Education.section 509(b) of Pub. L. 96–88section 3508(b) of Title 20
